title: Biere & Code Paris
description: Beer, code, and good conversation. A weekly meetup for developers, engineers, and tech enthusiasts.

nextEvent.title: Weekly Meetup
nextEvent.vibe: Different bar each week - check Meetup for this week's spot
nextEvent.description: Tech talks, project demos, and open networking. All levels welcome.

firstTime.title: First Time?
firstTime.subtitle: Here's What to Expect
points: No registration needed - just show up and introduce yourself
All skill levels welcome, from beginners to experts
Relaxed atmosphere: bring projects, questions, or just curiosity
Make friends, find collaborators, or simply enjoy good conversation

community.title: Latest from the Community
community.description: Event recaps, member spotlights, and project showcases.

monthly.title: Weekly Meetups
monthly.description: Every Thursday, we gather for tech talks, project demos, and open networking. Bring your projects, questions, or just your curiosity.

updates.title: Community Updates
updates.description: Event recaps, member spotlights, tech trends, and project showcases. Weekly posts keep you connected to what our community is building and learning together.

community.title: Open to All
community.description: All languages, frameworks, and experience levels welcome. From students to senior engineers, career-switchers to founders - if you're curious about tech, you belong here.

learnTogether.title: Learn Together
learnTogether.description: Share knowledge, discuss emerging technologies, and explore diverse perspectives. From debugging sessions to architecture debates, we grow by learning from each other.

relax.title: Relax & Connect
relax.description: Tech doesn't have to be intense. Meet in a casual setting where you can be yourself, build genuine friendships, and find your work-life balance in the community.

network.title: Grow Your Network
network.description: Connect with fellow developers, discover collaboration opportunities, find mentors or mentees. Some of Paris's best tech friendships started over a beer.

about.title: About Us
about.description: Meet the team behind Biere & Code Paris.

story.title: Our Story
story.description: What started as two friends grabbing a beer and talking about code has grown into one of Paris's most welcoming tech communities. Every Thursday, 30+ developers, engineers, and tech enthusiasts meet to share ideas, demo projects, and connect over craft beer. No corporate agenda, no gatekeeping - just people who love building things.

venue.title: Where We Meet
venue.description: We try a different bar every week across Paris. Check our Meetup page for this week's spot. The only constant: good beer and good conversation.

raph.bio: Senior Developer Evangelist at Defang and a serial entrepreneur with deep roots in the startup ecosystem. As the founder of Ephemere Creative and Tembo Tech, Raph combines technical expertise with a passion for community building and mentorship, bridging the gap between complex cloud technologies and the developers who use them.

miguel.bio: Senior Software Engineer at Gencove (NYC), specializing in high-scale Cloud Infrastructure and Backend systems for genomic analysis. An active open-source contributor and former digital nomad, Miguel brings a global perspective and strong entrepreneurial background to engineering, having built and launched successful products across multiple platforms.

footer.mission: Building a welcoming tech community in Paris, one beer at a time.
footer.builtWith: Built with love by the community

aiOrchestration.title: Biere & Code: AI Orchestration
aiOrchestration.tagline: A new event in planning for Paris builders
aiOrchestration.description: We are exploring a new Biere & Code event focused on AI orchestration: agents, multi-step workflows, evaluation, tooling, and real deployment stories. This page is intentionally simple because the format is still being shaped.
aiOrchestration.coBrandingNote: A specialized initiative by Biere & Code
aiOrchestration.heroCtaDescription: Use Meetup to show interest while we shape the first session.

themes.title: What we want to cover
themes.description: The goal is practical discussion, not abstract hype. We want a first event that helps builders compare approaches, share tradeoffs, and learn from real systems.

cards.title: Agents and workflow design
cards.description: How people structure multi-step systems, decide where agents help, and keep the workflow understandable when complexity starts to grow.

cards.title: Evaluation and operational reality
cards.description: What actually breaks in production: flaky tools, latency, observability gaps, prompt drift, retries, and handoff failures between components.

cards.title: Demos, case studies, and code
cards.description: Short demos, architecture walk-throughs, and examples from real projects instead of broad product pitches.

firstEvent.title: What the first event may look like
firstEvent.description: We are designing the first session as a lightweight experiment, not as a fully formed sub-brand with a fixed cadence.
items: A compact evening format with one or two short talks and a discussion-heavy second half.
Examples from real builders using agents, orchestration tools, or custom workflow stacks.
Open Q&A around reliability, evaluation, tooling choices, and when orchestration is worth the cost.

audience.title: Who should come
points: Developers building agent workflows, internal tools, or production AI features.
People comparing frameworks and trying to understand where orchestration adds value.
Teams that care about debugging, evaluation, reliability, and shipping practical systems.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.a1: Not fully. This page is here so people can understand the direction and register interest while the first session is still being planned.
faq.q2: Do you already have a date?
faq.a2: Not yet. We are still testing demand, format, and scope before putting a date on the calendar.
faq.a3: Engineers, product builders, founders, and technical leads who are actively experimenting with agentic systems, orchestration, or evaluation.
faq.q4: Can I propose a topic or demo?
faq.a4: Yes. Join the Meetup community and mention that you would like to present, share a case study, or help shape the first session.

cta.title: Help us shape the first AI orchestration night
cta.description: If this topic is relevant to your work, follow the Meetup and keep an eye on community updates. That signal will help decide the first format, topics, and speakers.
